content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code
constraints: S consists of printable ASCII characters without spaces
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S (length ≥ 8). The string may contain any printable ASCII characters except newline.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character – the 8th character of S (1‑based indexing). Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only test case string is "CodeChef". Its 8th character (index 7) is 'f'.
For "HelloWorld" the 8th character is 'r'; for "abcdefghij" it is 'h'.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. For each of the T test cases read the whole line representing the string S. Because the required character is always at a fixed position (the 8th character, index 7 in 0‑based indexing) we can directly output S[7]. No additional processing, sorting or DP is needed. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and uses O(1) extra memory per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables besides the input buffer
